titleStmt.title: Letter from Marie Martin to Mary Martin, 27 September 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Marie Martin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Marie Martin (1892–1975) to her mother Mary Martin. The letter mainly refers to family members, including a brother Tommy who is fighting in the war. She also notes a rumour of the hospital may close for the winter. The letter ends with a request for two photos.Marie Helena Martin later known as 'Mother Mary', was a volunteer nurse during the First World War. She served in field hospitals in Malta, Leeds, and France working for the British army and later went on to become the founder of the Medical Missionaries of Mary (MMM).

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
B/F/1/65 27-9-16 My very dearest Mother, Just a very short
note to thank you for your letter.
Iam so glad to hear all is going
well with you all & that Desmond Maries's 9 year old brother
is getting on alright. Is Ritchie Marie's 13 year old brother
going back to Downside or are
you keeping him at home. I will be very nice if Cyril &
all are in the Army Class with
Jack but in one way I think
it would be just as well if they
were not all to gether as I do
not think any of that lot are
extra fond of workâ at the
same time it may make more
competition. Ihope Uncle Frank
is something better. I cannot
bear to think ofhim an invalide.
Oh if we could only get Tommy
homeâ I am feeling A1& the weather
has been gorgeous these last two
days, I am still spring cleaning
in empty Tents which is very
boring. We are living in a
very unsettle state here at present
one rumour is that this Hospital
will be close for the Winter as
it would be nearly impossible
to work in the tents. I wonder
what will happen I only know
we are not filling so quickly
as we expected. If you have
two of my Photos to spare please
send me two outâ Now must
end for to-day with my fondest
love to every one. Yours ever. Marie
